summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the OAuth introspection validator accepts tokens with a mismatched audience. A mock OAuth server returns an introspection response with audience https://wrong-resource.example.com, while the MCP server is configured to expect https://mcp-server.example.com. Despite this mismatch, the server accepts the token and processes the initialize request successfully.
expected_security_impact: The MCP server should reject the initialize request with HTTP 401 because the token's audience does not match the configured audience. Instead, the server returns HTTP 200 with a valid MCP initialize response, confirming complete authentication bypass.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the framework, started the mock OAuth introspection server and MCP server, sent an initialize request with a Bearer token whose audience was wrong-resource.example.com, and the MCP server returned HTTP 200 with a valid JSON-RPC initialize response, accepting the mismatched-audience token.
verification_method: The exploit first confirms that unauthenticated requests are rejected (HTTP 401), then sends a request with a Bearer token that is introspected by the mock server. The mock server returns an active token with audience https://wrong-resource.example.com while the MCP server is configured for https://mcp-server.example.com. If the server returns HTTP 200 (not 401), the audience validation is confirmed missing.
evidence_produced_by_run: Mock OAuth server received the introspection request and returned active=true with aud=https://wrong-resource.example.com.
MCP server accepted the initialize request with HTTP 200 despite the audience mismatch.
The exploit script confirmed the vulnerability by checking that the response status was 200 (not 401).

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
